name: Monstera epipremnoides
aboutText: Monstera epipremnoides is a remarkable species native to the humid premontane and lowland forests of Costa Rica and Panama. It thrives as a hemiepiphyte, climbing tree trunks with aerial roots. This plant is characterized by its long, elliptic to oblong leaves featuring conspicuous, elongated fenestrations arranged in orderly rows. The margins of the leaves are entire or only slightly undulate, and the stem is robust and climbing. It prefers warm, humid conditions with high rainfall and filtered light, which support its vigorous growth.

fieldNotes.title: The Impostor Name That Isn't One
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: The name means 'resembling Epipremnum,' which gives the game away: the deeply fenestrated, almost pinnate adult leaves vaguely suggest a mature Epipremnum pinnatum rather than a typical Monstera. This adult form is part of what makes it interesting — and identifying it correctly in juvenile form is genuinely challenging, since juvenile epipremnoides could plausibly be confused with several other species. Ultra rare in cultivation with correspondingly serious pricing. A Costa Rican to Panamanian endemic. Worth the pursuit for the dedicated Monstera collector.
